Portsmouth Middle School · STEM Department

Grow Food.
Change the
World.

Welcome to STEM & Challenge Academy — where 6th, 7th, and 8th graders engineer hydroponic systems, raise koi for aquaponics, and tackle real-world challenges that matter.

🐟 Aquaponics · Koi & Nutrients

Synthetic Nutrients
🌿
100% Student-Run
What We Do

Two Programs,
One Mission

Students in grades 6–8 build real skills — engineering, biology, problem-solving — while exploring food access and sustainability.

Grades 7 & 8
STEM Hydroponics & Aquaponics

Design, build, and operate living systems that grow real food — powered by koi fish instead of synthetic chemicals.

🌱 Hydroponics 🐟 Aquaponics 🧪 Nutrient Science 🥬 Food Systems
Grade 6
Challenge Academy

Open-ended challenges. No rulebook. Just your brain, your team, and the freedom to figure it out.

🥚 Egg Drop 🌉 Bridge Build 🏹 Catapult 🎤 Pecha Kucha
How It Works

From Koi Tank to Kitchen Table

Students learn the full cycle: how fish waste becomes plant fertilizer, how water circulates, and how we optimize systems for maximum growth — no synthetic nutrients required.

📍 Our Classroom System

See It in Action —
Built by Students

This is the actual hydroponic system students design, build, and operate right here at Portsmouth Middle School. Watch the nutrient channels, plant sites, and plumbing come together — all engineered by 7th and 8th graders.

No kits. No pre-made parts. Students spec out the PVC, wire the pumps, mix the nutrient solution, and troubleshoot problems as real engineers do.

🌱 Student-Built 💧 Recirculating System 🐟 Aquaponics-Ready 📐 PVC & PVC Fittings
🌿
Hydroponics

Plants grow in water-based nutrient solutions without soil. Students design and manage the systems, monitor pH and nutrient levels, and optimize for yield.

🐟
Aquaculture & Koi

Koi fish are raised in tanks. Students manage water quality, feeding, and fish health while learning how aquatic biology connects to plant nutrition.

♻️
Aquaponics Loop

Fish waste → bacteria → natural nitrates → plant food → clean water returns to fish. Students maintain and optimize this closed-loop ecosystem.

Food is a Right
Not a privilege
Our Why

Food Insecurity Is a Local Issue

  • Millions of families lack consistent access to fresh, nutritious food — even in communities like ours.
  • Hydroponics can grow food in warehouses, schools, and homes — bringing production directly to people who need it.
  • By eliminating synthetic nutrients through aquaponics, we model a more sustainable, accessible approach to growing food.
  • Students don't just study the problem — they prototype the solution, right here in class.
  • The skills students gain translate directly to real careers in food science, engineering, biology, and sustainable agriculture.
See My Classes →
Grade 6 · Challenge Academy

Your Challenge.
Your Solution.

No step-by-step instructions. No single right answer. Just a challenge, a team, and the freedom to figure it out — exactly like real engineers and scientists do.

01
🎤
Pecha Kucha

20 slides. 20 seconds each. Tell a compelling story about something you're passionate about. Fast-paced, visual, and unforgettable.

02
🥚
Egg Drop

Protect a raw egg from a multi-story drop using only the materials provided. Engineering, physics, and creativity — every design is different.

03
🌉
Bridge Building

Build the strongest bridge you can with limited materials. How much weight can your design hold before it fails? Iterate and find out.

04
🏹
Catapult

Build a catapult that launches as far and as accurately as possible. Physics meets craftsmanship in this classic engineering challenge.
